claim: Big Ass Prompts (BAPs)—10K to 1M token prompts filled with domain-specific data—unlock latent model capabilities because most engineers only send 2-3 sentence prompts and never exercise full context window.
actionable: Dump your entire codebase, database exports, OCR'd PDFs, or documentation into your prompt. Fill your context window. You'll discover capabilities you never knew existed in current models.
source_title: GPT-5 is coming: 3 ways to prepare for a 100x improvement in SOTA LLMs

claim: The prompt is the new fundamental unit of programming and knowledge work; mastering the prompt—not just code syntax—is how you prepare for 100x capability improvements.
actionable: Stop thinking of prompting as secondary to coding. Treat prompts with the same rigor as code: version them, test them, iterate on them, measure their quality.
source_title: GPT-5 is coming: 3 ways to prepare for a 100x improvement in SOTA LLMs

claim: Solving existing problems with current SOTA models + prompt chains proves you understand the landscape; 100x improvements will accelerate solutions you've already validated, not create entirely new capabilities.
actionable: Don't wait for better models. Aggressively attack problems you think are solvable now with current models. Document what works. When 100x arrives, you'll know exactly what to accelerate.
source_title: GPT-5 is coming: 3 ways to prepare for a 100x improvement in SOTA LLMs

claim: 100 prompts a day (100 P/D) builds expertise: prompt engineering is a skill that improves with deliberate repetition, same as coding. Without understanding current model limits, you won't recognize when 100x unlocks new territory.
actionable: Set a daily target of 100 prompt iterations. Use them to solve different problems, debug issues, test ideas. Track what works. Build your mental model of current-day LLM capabilities.
source_title: GPT-5 is coming: 3 ways to prepare for a 100x improvement in SOTA LLMs

claim: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) is a problem that future models will solve; focus on BAPs and context-filling now instead of over-investing in RAG infrastructure.
actionable: Before building a RAG pipeline, try filling your prompt with raw data. Only optimize with RAG if your knowledge base is too large to fit in context or if prompt latency becomes prohibitive.
source_title: GPT-5 is coming: 3 ways to prepare for a 100x improvement in SOTA LLMs
